name: entityOS
title: entityOS — A cloud operating system for entities.
description: Public marketing and security site for entityOS, the cloud operating system for individuals, organisations, collectives, and DAOs. Built by ibCom (Australia). Covers the platform pillars, a staged 'closed internet' security model, and a security reward (responsible disclosure) program.
tagline: A cloud operating system for entities.

pillars.name: Cloud
pillars.summary: 700+ API methods for any entity type — the core platform.

pillars.name: Continuity
pillars.summary: Automation engine for workflows and event-driven processing.

pillars.name: SSI
pillars.summary: Self-sovereign identity for verifiable credentials (KERI / ACDC).

pillars.name: On-Chain
pillars.summary: Cardano blockchain integration for tokens and decentralised storage.

pillars.name: AI
pillars.summary: AI agent integration and automation on the entityOS platform.

pages.title: Homepage
pages.summary: Overview of entityOS: hero, entity types, the five pillars, the staged security model, tools, open-source repos, and getting started.

pages.title: Security Stage 1 — IP Allowlisting
pages.summary: Network-layer access control: restrict who can reach services by IP. The essential first boundary.

pages.title: Security Stage 2 — mTLS + X.509
pages.summary: Cryptographic per-connection identity layered over network controls using mutual TLS and X.509 certificates.

pages.title: Why the Closed Internet — The Moment
pages.summary: The case for treating the public internet as hostile, driven by AI-accelerated threat capability.

pages.title: The New Mindset
pages.summary: The shift in security thinking behind the closed-internet model.

pages.title: The entityOS Solution
pages.summary: How entityOS answers the closed-internet problem.

pages.title: Implementation — Stages 1 & 2
pages.summary: How entityOS implements IP allowlisting and mTLS + X.509 in practice.

pages.title: Security Reward Program
pages.summary: Responsible-disclosure and reward program. Report vulnerabilities through a form and earn a reward scaled to impact.

reward_program.amounts_are: indicative; final reward at entityOS's discretion based on severity, exploitability, report quality, and impact

tiers.examples: Allowlist/mTLS bypass, RCE, SSI credential forgery, cross-tenant data exposure
Authentication bypass, IAM privilege escalation, certificate validation flaw
Stored XSS in a sensitive context, IDOR with limited data, meaningful logic flaw

in_scope: Closed-internet edge (IP allowlisting, network isolation, staged access)
Mutual TLS & PKI (X.509 issuance and validation)
Self-sovereign identity (KERI / ACDC credential handling, key state)
Serverless factory code (Lambda factories, API Gateway, IAM boundaries)
Data & allowlist storage (S3 object access, allowlist persistence, audit trails)
out_of_scope: Volumetric or rate-limit denial of service
Social engineering of staff, users, or partners
Automated scanner output without a working proof of concept
Outdated-dependency reports with no demonstrated exploit path

tools.name: ORG

ecosystem.role: Core platform and main marketing site

agent_guidance.summary: entityos.io is a public, read-only marketing and security site. There are no transactional endpoints to call here.
allowed: Direct security researchers to the reward program at /security/reward and the report form
Direct developers to the Console, Docs, and Learn properties to sign up or build
do_not: Attempt to bypass, probe, or test IP allowlisting, mTLS, or other access controls without authorisation — do so only within the reward program's scope and rules
Submit reports by email; use the report form
Treat indicative reward amounts as guaranteed payouts
